projects.name: Hierarch
projects.description: React task management app with drag-and-drop, focus timer, and today view
projects.notes: A comprehensive Design System page is live at /design-system, publicly accessible without auth, documenting all colors, typography, spacing, radii, elevation, buttons, inputs, badges, cards, icons, phases, statuses, and artifact types. Pushed 13 accumulated commits to GitHub covering months of feature work. Attempted Figma capture of screens via MCP but z-index conflicts with modals need resolution. Next: re-enable Linear webhook HMAC, submit Figma app for review, upgrade Supabase to Pro, build Insights view, make primary action buttons consistent.

projects.name: Kinlo
projects.description: iOS health app for desk workers. Tracks workouts, water intake, and movement breaks tied to work hours. Streak-based habit reinforcement. Social gamification via friend leaderboard and challenges (Phase 2). Phase 1 MVP complete.
projects.notes: Kinlo has undergone a full principal-level code audit and refactor touching 34 files. The app now has a design system (KinloTheme), tab-based navigation, fixed security issues (entitlements, debug buttons removed, @MainActor thread safety), working notification intervals with quiet hours, a rebuilt RecommendationsView, proper error handling, and accessibility support. Fixed a SwiftUI hang on the Work Hours onboarding step caused by wheel pickers + withAnimation conflicts. Next: test the Xcode build end-to-end, remove stale Core Data references from pbxproj, design the app icon, and begin Phase 2 social features.

projects.name: NourishAI
projects.notes: NourishAI Expo prototype is complete and running in the iOS simulator with auth, onboarding, AI meal generation, water tracking, and product scanner. EAS Build kept failing due to Reanimated/Worklets version conflicts. Decision made to pivot to native SwiftUI using the existing Kinlo Xcode project instead of fighting Expo build issues. The Kinlo project already has auth, onboarding, Supabase, and MVVM architecture. Next session: adapt Kinlo's SwiftUI codebase to include NourishAI's 6 lifestyle pillars (Eat, Move, Hydrate, Rest, Scan, Rhythm) with AI meal plans, macro tracking, food logging (barcode + photo + manual), and EU-banned product scanner.

projects.name: RockPass
projects.notes: Full Phase 1 scaffold is complete and running in Xcode on iPhone 16 Pro simulator. Built 5 screens (Home, Gyms, My Pass, Gear, Account) in Expo Router with a custom elevated center tab bar, Google Maps integration with a live API key restricted to com.rockpass.app, and a 909-line Figma plugin ready to generate all screens in the RockPass Figma file. Next: run the Figma plugin, wire up real gym data via Google Places API, and build the auth layer.

projects.name: hierarch-fos
projects.notes: Hierarch FOS marketing site is live at tryhierarch.com. All login/signup links now point to hierarchical.app. Feature sections simplified from 10 total cards to 7, integrations updated to reflect Linear + Figma (live) and Jira (coming soon), testimonials cleaned up. Next: connect tryhierarch.com domain properly, add real pricing tiers, build out remaining placeholder pages.

projects.name: mission-control
projects.description: Claude Code command center — live dashboard tracking projects, skills, MCPs, and session history
projects.notes: Mission Control now has a Launch button on every project card that opens the project in Cursor with full context. The API route reads the project's mission-log.md and .mc.json, writes combined context into .cursor/rules/mission-context.mdc, then opens Cursor. Next: scaffold mission-log.md templates across projects and test the end-to-end launch flow.

projects.name: portfolio
projects.notes: AI chat homepage at / with interactive dot matrix bg, dark/light mode, and streaming Claude Haiku responses. Portfolio at /portfolio behind Supabase access code gate with admin panel (/admin) for code management, metrics, and password changes. Hierarch and Prompt-to-Email case studies added. Project cards on chat page use static images. Gate page redirects to intended destination after auth. Mobile optimized with responsive breakpoints, stacked project cards, and iOS zoom prevention. All deployed to aaroncougle.com. Next: add DMARC case study, continue mobile polish, add images to Prompt-to-Email case study.

projects.name: prouxkit
projects.notes: All 9 tools built and styled with light mode Coolors-inspired design. Custom wrench logo with favicon. Deployed to Vercel with prouxkit.com domain configured.

projects.name: radar
projects.notes: Radar is fully migrated from Supabase to Neon Postgres via the Vercel integration, freeing up a Supabase project slot. All 10 API routes rewritten to use @neondatabase/serverless with raw SQL, 436 companies and 7 active job listings migrated, and the app is deployed and scanning on Neon. Next: delete the old Supabase project and optionally set up a custom domain.

projects.name: showcase
projects.notes: Showcase is live at showcase.aaroncougle.com with 4 slides using a clean flex-based bento layout — 2/3 hero on the left, stacked thumbnails on the right on desktop. Mobile stacks them vertically with a 250px hero on top and a horizontal thumbnail row below. The site is fully deployed and polished; no pending tasks.

projects.name: website-analyzer
projects.description: Next.js tool for analyzing and auditing websites

projects.name: workhistory
projects.description: Work history visualizer + AI resume generator
projects.notes: Standalone dev app for the work history tool. Three tabs: Profile, Work History, Resumes. Changes made here get synced to ~/Apps/portfolio/ for production. Next: resume polish, more profile variants.
